data.title: META or MSFT: Which Is the Better Tech Stock Post-Earnings?
data.description:
Tech giants Meta Platforms (META) and Microsoft (MSFT) both reported December quarter results on January 28. Meta impressed investors with a stronger-than-expected sales outlook for Q1FY26, while Microsoft's slower cloud growth and softer margin guidance disappointed. Subsequently, META stock is rallying in pre-market trading and MSFT shares are dropping. Despite the different investor perspectives, Wall Street remains bullish on the long-term prospects of both companies, assigning a "Strong Buy" consensus.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Meta's Blockbuster Results

Meta Platforms, owner of Facebook and Instagram, beat analysts' Q4FY25 expectations with earnings per share (EPS) of $8.88, surpassing the $8.23 consensus estimate. Revenue jumped 24% year-over-year to $59.89 billion, exceeding the $58.59 billion forecast.

Additionally, Meta's daily active people (DAP) on its social media apps totaled 3.58 billion, meeting estimates. Meta forecast 2026 total expenses between $162 billion and $169 billion, with a big chunk allocated to its AI initiatives and infrastructure expansion.

Microsoft's Azure Growth Disappoints

Microsoft's adjusted EPS hit $4.14, surpassing the $3.91 consensus estimate. Revenue reached $81.27 billion, up 17% year-over-year and topping the $80.31 billion forecast. However, its Intelligent Cloud division, including Azure, reported sales of $32.9 billion, modestly beating the consensus of $32.2 billion, while analysts had expected a higher beat.

Microsoft is grappling with AI capacity constraints as demand surges faster than supply, capping near-term revenue potential. To tackle this, CFO Amy Hood highlighted last year's $88.2 billion in capital spending and announced plans for a substantial increase in fiscal 2026.

META Wins Analyst Price Target Boosts

Wells Fargo analyst Ken Gawrelski lifted his META price target from $754 to $849, implying nearly 27% upside potential. He noted that the upbeat revenue guidance, with notable Q1 acceleration, offsets elevated OpEx and CapEx projections. Earnings estimates for 2026/2027 remain steady, though stronger growth should support an expanded multiple.

Moreover, Morgan Stanley analyst Brian Nowak raised his price target to $825 (from $750), suggesting 23.4% upside potential. He boosted Meta's FY27 EPS estimate by 10%, citing Meta's investments in engagement and monetization. Nowak highlighted that these efforts are fueling the fastest growth in over four years, when the business was roughly half its current size.

Also, Jefferies analyst Brent Thill boosted his price target from $910 to $1,000, implying 49.5% upside. He noted that Meta delivered strong revenue upside, with AI-driven growth reacceleration validating returns on its massive investment cycle. Thill added that Meta provided several data points showing AI powering its core business flywheel.

Analysts Trim MSFT Price Targets

Wells Fargo analyst Michael Turrin cut his price target from $630 to $615, implying 27.7% upside potential. Despite Azure capacity constraint concerns, Turrin views the Q3 guidance as signaling improved growth potential in the second half of 2026 and beyond with added capacity.

Similarly, Citi analyst Tyler Radke lowered his price target to $635 (from $660), implying 31.8% upside. He stated that Azure showed slight upside but growth slowed marginally due to capacity allocation prioritizing first-party services. While Radke trimmed Azure estimates post-earnings, he highlighted accelerating momentum for Copilot.

Meanwhile, Morgan Stanley analyst Keith Weiss maintained his $650 price target but removed Microsoft stock from his "Top Pick" list. He noted that investor focus zeroed in on GenAI indicators like Azure growth (38% cc, just 1 point above guidance) and M365 Commercial Cloud, missing hopes for a 2%-3% beat.

Which Is Better: Meta or Microsoft?

Based on TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool, both Meta and Microsoft have a Strong Buy rating, with META shares offering a modestly higher upside potential over the next twelve months. META also has a higher TipRanks Smart Score of eight, which implies it can outperform market expectations.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: AAPL Earnings: Apple Stock Rises after Smashing Estimates
data.description:
Shares of Apple (AAPL) rose in after-hours trading after the tech giant reported earnings for its first quarter of Fiscal Year 2026. Earnings per share came in at $2.84, which beat analysts' consensus estimate of $2.68 per share. Sales increased by 15.7% year-over-year, with revenue hitting $143.76 billion. This also beat analysts' expectations of $138.39 billion.

Is AAPL Stock a Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on AAPL stock based on 19 Buys, 11 Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average AAPL price target of $298.84 per share implies 15.8% upside potential. However, it's worth noting that estimates will likely change following today's earnings report.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Lose Steam on Tech Weakness as Trump Prepares Fed Chair Pick
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed lower on Thursday as tech and software stocks sold off sharply.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Microsoft (MSFT) closed with a 9.9% loss despite reporting higher-than-expected revenue and adjusted earnings per share (EPS) for the period ended December 31. Microsoft joined a broad selloff in software stocks, with ServiceNow (NOW), Salesforce (CRM), and Workday (WDAY) also posting steep drawdowns. Investors remain concerned about the disruptive effects of AI that could potentially intensify competition in the software space and lower margins.

During a Cabinet meeting, President Trump said that he would announce his nominee for the Fed Chair position "sometime next week." Trump ramped up criticism of Fed Chair Jerome Powell after the central bank voted 10-2 on Wednesday to hold rates steady between 3.50% and 3.75%. Powell's term is set to end on May 15, although his term as a Governor will last until January 31, 2028.

"He is hurting our Country, and its National Security," Trump said in a Truth Social post on Thursday. "We should have a substantially lower rate now that even this moron admits inflation is no longer a problem or threat."

BlackRock's (BLK) Rick Rieder is the front-runner to be nominated by Trump to lead the Fed, according to prediction platform Polymarket. Rieder holds 38% odds, followed by former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh at 30% and current Fed Governor Christopher Waller at 14%.

Before the market open, the Department of Labor (DOL) announced that initial jobless claims fell by 1,000 to 209,000, above the consensus estimate of 205,000. In addition, the DOL revised the prior week's claims to 210,000 from 200,000. Initial jobless claims are an indicator of layoffs and track how many people have applied for unemployment benefits for the first time. The data comes after Amazon (AMZN) announced it would lay off 16,000 employees, bringing its total since October to 30,000.

Continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by one week and track the number of people continuing to receive unemployment benefits, fell by 38,000 to 1.827 million and were below the estimate of 1.850 million. However, the DOL revised the prior week's figure to 1.865 million from 1.849 million.

Elsewhere, the U.S. trade deficit for November nearly doubled to $56.8 billion from $29.2 billion in October, driven by a 5% rise in imports to $348.9 billion. Rising imports are a headwind to gross domestic product (GDP), as they reduce net exports.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.13% loss, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) fell by 0.53%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: V Earnings: Visa Stock Falls Despite Beating Wall Street Estimates
data.description:
The stock of Visa (V) is down 2% in after hours trading despite the credit card giant's financial results beating Wall Street estimates on the top and bottom lines.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

San Francisco-based Visa announced earnings per share (EPS) of $3.17, which was ahead of the $3.14 consensus forecast among analysts. Revenue in what was the company's fiscal first quarter totaled $10.90 billion, which was ahead of the $10.70 billion expected on Wall Street. Sales were up 15% from a year ago.

Management attributed the strong quarter to resilient consumer spending and a strong year-end holiday season, with payments volume rising 8% year-over-year. International, or cross-border, volumes grew 11% from the same quarter in 2024, while total processed transactions increased 9% to 69.4 billion.

"Visa delivered a very strong fiscal first quarter with net revenue up 15% year-over-year, GAAP EPS up 17% and non-GAAP EPS up 15%," said Ryan McInerney, Chief Executive Officer of Visa. "Our purposeful investments in our Visa as a Service stack continue to position us as a payments hyperscaler to deliver technology and infrastructure that redefine what's possible in payments."

The company's service revenue rose 13% to $4.8 billion, while data processing revenue increased 17% to $5.5 billion. International transaction revenue grew 6% to $3.7 billion, and other revenue jumped 33% to $1.2 billion.

During the quarter, Visa repurchased approximately 11 million shares at an average cost of $342.13 per share, totaling $3.8 billion. The company's board also declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.67 per share, payable on March 2.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: "Real Time Sports Conversations": Warner Bros. Discovery Stock (NASDAQ:WBD) Slips Despite Winter Olympics Plans
data.description:
While we have been following the news of entertainment giant Warner Bros. Discovery's (WBD) potential sale to somebody else, Warner continues to operate, and it has some exciting plans ahead for one of the biggest events in sports: the Winter Games. The Olympics are a big opportunity for sports channels, and Warner is no exception, setting up deals with TikTok and X for coverage. This did not sit well with investors, oddly, who sent shares down fractionally in the closing minutes of Thursday's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Warner will offer up some special content—particularly behind-the-scenes content—for X to air, as well as some key highlights from Winter Olympics events. The special features will be added to X channels and given priority on X feeds. They will also be localized for the various European markets that would see the content in question.

This is roughly similar to the partnership established for the last Paris Olympics, which offered some impressive exposure numbers. X claimed that the Warner content from those Olympics pulled in 2.4 trillion impressions, 8.2 billion sports posts, 221 billion video views and more. The result will create "real time sports conversations" going on at the same time as the sports events, making for a more immersive performance that will hopefully improve ad revenues.

Trouble With DC Fans

DC comics fans are a little less than pleased with Warner as far as streaming content goes. Warner has been pursuing a policy of actively selling streaming rights for multiple titles to other streaming platforms. This has left DC fans in an odd position: Warner was the streaming home for DC, but currently, HBO Max has less than half of DC's total content available.

One of the biggest problems is DC animation. DC released several titles that were more adult-oriented, but were also animated. Titles like Justice League: A New Frontier and Batman: Gotham by Gaslight were sold to other streaming platforms. This may just sound like sour grapes, but reports suggest that some subscribers are canceling their subscriptions over the lack of content available. This represents a very real potential problem in a time when streaming is the new cable.

Is WBD Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on WBD stock based on five Buys and 11 Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 163.47% rally in its share price over the past year, the average WBD price target of $25.98 per share implies 5.84% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Is the Gold Rally Over? Not So Fast, Says UBS
data.description:
Gold's (XAUUSD) historic rally has led to concern for some investors, who expect the precious metal's momentum to run dry. On the other side of the argument is UBS, which raised its gold price target for March, June, and September to $6,200 from $5,000.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

UBS expects the upside to be driven by central bank demand, strong exchange-traded fund (ETF) inflows, and more purchases of physical gold. The investment firm's bull case sees gold jumping to $7,200, while its bear case estimates a price of $4,600, with both targets representing a roughly one standard deviation move.

Where Will Gold Trade By the End of 2026?

At the same time, UBS expects the metal to end 2026 at $5,900, which still implies upside of about 10% from current levels. Factors to consider include whether the Fed is hawkish or dovish and the scope of geopolitical tensions.

UBS joins a growing chorus of Wall Street firms with high expectations. Earlier this week, both Deutsche Bank and Societe Generale raised their year-end price targets to $6,000.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX and xAI Are Reportedly in Talks to Merge
data.description:
Elon Musk's SpaceX and xAI are reportedly in talks to merge ahead of a major public offering planned for later this year, according to Reuters. This is noteworthy because the combined company would bring together SpaceX's rockets, Starlink satellites, the X social media platform, and xAI's Grok chatbot under one company. While the talks are still confidential and not finalized, the move could strengthen SpaceX's push into building data centers in space.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Under the proposed deal, xAI shareholders would receive SpaceX shares, and two new entities have already been created in Nevada to help facilitate the transaction. However, the exact value, timing, and structure of the merger are still unclear, and no final agreement has been signed. Some xAI executives may also have the option to receive cash instead of SpaceX stock if the deal goes through. The main reasons for the possible merger are artificial intelligence and infrastructure.

Through xAI, Musk is building a massive AI supercomputer called Colossus and has argued that putting AI data centers in space, powered by solar energy, could significantly reduce costs within a few years. In addition, bringing xAI into SpaceX could improve SpaceX's chances of winning more U.S. defense contracts, as the Pentagon increases its use of AI systems. Interestingly, SpaceX is already the world's most valuable private company, last valued at about $800 billion, and has lined up banks for an IPO that could value it at $1.5 trillion.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

When it comes to Elon Musk's companies, most of them are privately held. However, retail investors can invest in his most popular company, Tesla (TSLA). Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 11 Buys, 11 Holds, and six Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $401.27 per share implies 4.1% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Boeing Stock (NYSE:BA) Slides as Supply Chain Problems Mount
data.description:
Aerospace giant Boeing (BA) has what may be a growing problem on its hands. It is eager to build aircraft, and that is a good thing, especially given the sheer scope of its backlog. But while its spirit is willing, its flesh may not be up to the challenge. In fact, there are mounting concerns about Boeing's supply chain in a time when Boeing most needs it running at its fullest. Boeing shares slipped over 1.5% in Thursday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Boeing had a staggering earnings report, reports noted. Analysts were expecting a $0.39 per share loss, but Boeing blasted that out of the water by posting a $9.92 per share profit. Though this figure was generated mostly by a hefty one-time gain, the fact that the gain was over 25 times the figure of its projected loss had to hit home.

Boeing will need all hands on deck, though, as its supply chain starts looking stressed. Boeing recently set up a deal with USA Rare Earth that will give Boeing access to the minerals it needs to make everything from navigation systems to engines. However, if anything goes wrong with this deal, Boeing will have to turn to markets mostly run by the Chinese, which could add considerable pressure.

New Helicopter Project

And, even as Boeing works to work out its supply chain ahead of a major new production expansion, Boeing also faces a new contract from the United States Air Force for four more MH-139A helicopters. This is the second such contract it has received in the last five months, reports note, and the total value of such contracts now stands at over $262 million.

The MH-139 program's director, Azeem Khan, noted, "The quick succession of contracts demonstrates that the U.S. Air Force is all in on bringing the MH-139A capability to the warfighter as quickly as possible. Our team is dedicated to delivering and maintaining these aircraft, driven by a commitment to excellence, where safety and quality are our top priorities."

Is Boeing a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BA stock based on 14 Buys, one Hold and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 34.57% rally in its share price over the past year, the average BA price target of $268.69 per share implies 13.51% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Trump to Name Fed Chair Next Week as BlackRock's Rieder Leads the Odds
data.description:
President Trump announced on Thursday that he would name his nominee for the Fed Chair position "sometime next week."

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

BlackRock's (BLK) Chief Investment Officer of Global Fixed Income, Rick Rieder, is the front-runner to be selected, according to prediction platform Polymarket. Rieder leads with 38% odds, followed by former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh at 29% and current Fed Governor Christopher Waller at 14%.

Trump Calls for Aggressive Rate Cuts

On Wednesday, the Fed voted 10-2 to maintain interest rates, much to the ire of Trump. "We should have a substantially lower rate now that even this moron admits inflation is no longer a problem or threat," Trump said in a Truth Social post earlier today, referencing Fed Chair Jerome Powell. He added that lower rates could save America billions of dollars in interest payments and that tariffs support the rationale of the U.S. having the lowest interest rate in the world.

Powell's term as Chair is set to end on May 15, although his role as a Governor extends until January 31, 2028.
